page: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The instance is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The high clock speed of 4.2 GHz drives strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2333.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression results. However, because the instance is constrained to a single physical core, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the poor and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests also show limitations, with uncached read-only large block bandwidth measured at 21.59 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4-2ms_v2 offers a high density of RAM relative to its compute footprint, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring large memory capacities without massive parallel processing needs. It is suited for memory-intensive applications with low parallelization requirements, such as small-scale in-memory databases, single-threaded data processing tasks, and text processing workloads. It is less suited for highly parallelized compute tasks, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to its single physical core limitation.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core operating at 4.2 GHz, paired with 84.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports premium storage and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2333.0 and strong single-threaded compression. However, multi-core performance is weak due to the single physical core constraint, and memory bandwidth is relatively low. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded workloads, such as small in-memory databases or text processing, but is not suited for highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 84.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio for specialized single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 260 MB L3 cache
84.0 GB of system memory providing 42.0 GB per physical core
Top-tier single-core performance with strong single-threaded compression benchmarks
Limited multi-core scaling due to single physical core architecture
No local storage or bundled GPUs, with support for premium remote storage
Best suited for memory-intensive, low-parallelization workloads and text processing
